concerns.text: Tasks T028-T034 (Reviewer Concerns: Self-Prediction Penalty, Metabolic Cost Proxy, Irreducibility Test, Falsification Criterion, Simulation vs. Phenomenon) implement features with no corresponding Functional Requirements (FR-NNN) or Success Criteria (SC-NNN) in spec.md. These represent unapproved scope creep; the spec explicitly limits scope to measurable metrics (self-consistency, calibration, error detection) and does not authorize these philosophical operationalizations.

Task T004 mandates fetching 'GSM8K, MMLU, and Pile' datasets. However, spec FR-002 and US-01 explicitly require training only on the 'arXiv subset of the Pile dataset'. The inclusion of GSM8K and MMLU in the data loading task is unanchored to any training requirement and contradicts the specific dataset scope defined in FR-002.

Task T024 implements 'paired t-tests, Cohen's d, and Bonferroni correction' but omits the explicit implementation of '95% confidence intervals' required by spec SC-004 and Constitution Principle VI. The task description does not cover the full set of statistical outputs mandated by the spec.

Task T019 describes generating '10 reasoning paths' generally for the benchmark runner. Spec FR-003 specifically mandates this exact count for the 'Self-Consistency benchmark evaluation' (distinct from MMLU/GSM8K in the spec's logic). The task lacks the specific anchoring to the Self-Consistency subset required to satisfy FR-003.

Task T014 instructs to 'Add validation to prevent recursion depth > 2'. Spec Edge Cases explicitly mandate that if the recursion depth causes an OOM, the system 'MUST fail the run... and MUST NOT automatically reduce the recursion depth'. The task description is ambiguous and does not explicitly cover the required hard-fail/exit behavior, risking a violation of the negative constraint.
